ddls:
CREATE TABLE "films" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the film */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the film */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the film */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the film */,
  "director" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "producer" TEXT /* Name of the producer */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "box_office" TEXT /* Box office revenue of the film */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the film in minutes */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Average rating of the film */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of production */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language of the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the actor */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the actor */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the actor */,
  "place_of_birth" TEXT /* Place of birth of the actor */,
  "height" TEXT /* Height of the actor in meters */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Notable awards the actor has received */,
  "social_media_handle" TEXT /* Social media handle of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the character */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the character */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the character */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the character */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the character */,
  "backstory" TEXT /* Brief backstory of the character */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_actor" (
  "film_actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film-actor relationship */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the actor's role in the film */,
  "role_type" TEXT /* Type of role (e.g., lead, supporting, cameo) */,
  "screen_time" INTEGER /* Screen time of the actor in minutes */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Awards the actor received for this role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_actor_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_actor_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_actor_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "note" TEXT /* Note or comment about the film */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the note */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the note was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the note */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_notes_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_notes_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_notes_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "character_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "note" TEXT /* Note or comment about the character */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the note */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the note was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the note */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_notes_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_notes_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_notes_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, casting director, production team) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film being accessed */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actor_filmography" (
  "filmography_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each filmography entry */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the film was released */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the actor's performance */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Notable awards the actor received for their performance in the film */,
  "notable_scenes" TEXT /* Notable scenes the actor was part of in the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("filmography_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actor_filmography_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actor_filmography_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actor_filmography_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)
